sanskrit: नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः । उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः ॥
hindiTranslation: टिप्पणी प0 55 असत् का तो भाव सत्ता विद्यमान नहीं है और सत् का अभाव विद्यमान नहीं है, तत्त्वदर्शी महापुरुषोंने इन दोनोंका ही अन्त अर्थात् तत्त्व देखा है ॥
englishTranslation: The unreal has no being; the real has no nonbeing. Seers have understood the boundary of both.
essence: What passes away was never ultimate; what is real cannot vanish.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: What is temporary does not truly exist, and what is real cannot be destroyed. Those who see clearly understand the difference between the two.
simpleInsight: You can stop treating every passing fear or loss as ultimate. Some things vanish; what is real stays.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen before the battle. Krishna has already told him to endure changing sensations; now he goes deeper, showing that the wise see through appearance itself. This verse defines what can truly be lost and what cannot.
modernRelevance: You reread a message that stung, then imagine the whole relationship collapsing. Krishna’s point cuts through that spiral: not every passing state deserves the weight you give it.
translationLiteral: नासतः (of the unreal) / विद्यते (exists) / भावः (being) / न (not) / अभावः (nonbeing) / विद्यते (exists) / सतः (of the real) / उभयोः (of both) / अपि (also) / दृष्टः (seen) / अन्तः (limit) / तु (indeed) / अनयोः (of these) / तत्त्वदर्शिभिः (by seers of reality)
insight: It shifts attention from appearances to what remains unchanged beneath them.

quoteVariants.text: The unreal never is. The real never ceases.
The real remains. The unreal passes.
